---
title: "CG3CG3"
author: 
- de Pedro, Joenell Conrad
- Florentino, Ian Miguel
- Nacu, Drixyl Reece
- Ruelan, Ivan Paul
- Seno, Matthew Anakin
date: "CG3CG3"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
data <- read.csv("./src/Culminating_Activity/AI_Impact_Student_Life_2026.csv")
head(data)
```

# Preprocessing data

Apparently you don't have to convert categorical into numerical 
values in R, it just automatically labels them with the as.factor function

```{r}
data <- data[, -1]
# Non Ordinal
data$Major = as.factor(data$Major)
data$Primary_AI_Tool = as.factor(data$Primary_AI_Tool)
data$Main_Usage_Case = as.factor(data$Main_Usage_Case)
data$Main_Usage_Case = as.factor(data$Main_Usage_Case)

# Ordinal Ranking
data$AI_Ethics_Concern <- factor(
  data$AI_Ethics_Concern,
  levels = c("Low", "Medium", "High"),
  ordered = TRUE
)
head(data)
```
